title: RX thread error backoff resets consecutive_errors counter on -EAGAIN but not on ret==0
description:
Location: lora_l2.c:163-183

**What goes wrong:** The RX loop handles three cases:
1. ret < 0 && ret == -EAGAIN: resets consecutive_errors (line 166), continue
2. ret < 0 && ret != -EAGAIN: increments consecutive_errors, sleeps 1s, continue
3. ret == 0: continue (empty packet) - does NOT reset consecutive_errors
4. ret > 0: resets consecutive_errors at line 179

If the radio alternates between errors and empty packets (ret==0), the consecutive_errors counter never resets, eventually triggering the hardware warning spuriously.

**Trigger:** Radio returning empty packets interspersed with transient errors.

**Fix:** Reset consecutive_errors on ret==0 as well, or document that empty packets don't count as successful receives.
